description: Hands introducing squeeze play — recognizing potential, rectifying the count, managing entries and threats, and executing simple, positional, automatic, and double squeezes.

hands.title: 216. Recognizing squeeze potential
hands.notes: Count 11 top tricks. West guards both hearts and diamonds. Running spades squeezes West — he cannot hold both stoppers.

hands.title: 217. Count winners for squeeze position
hands.notes: Verify you are one trick short with all remaining winners. This is the N-1 condition for a squeeze to operate.

hands.title: 218. Rectify the count
hands.notes: You must lose one trick early to rectify the count. Duck a heart, then run winners to squeeze West in hearts and diamonds.

hands.title: 219. Cash winners in proper order
hands.notes: Cash spades before diamonds. Running diamonds first squanders the squeeze — the order of winners matters to preserve threats and entries.

hands.title: 220. Simple squeeze against one defender
hands.notes: West alone guards hearts and diamonds. Run spades and clubs — on the last winner, West is squeezed and must unguard one suit.

hands.title: 221. Positional squeeze basics
hands.notes: The squeeze only works because West sits over the threat cards. If East held those guards, the positional squeeze would fail.

hands.title: 222. Automatic squeeze basics
hands.notes: Threat cards are split so that either defender is squeezed regardless of position. The automatic squeeze works against either opponent.

hands.title: 223. Squeeze against a defender guarding two suits
hands.notes: West is overloaded guarding hearts and diamonds. Run black-suit winners to force West to abandon one guard.

hands.title: 224. Entry management for squeeze
hands.notes: Keep an entry to dummy's threat card. The squeeze card is useless if you cannot reach the established winner after defender discards.

hands.title: 225. Avoid blocking before squeeze
hands.notes: Do not cash the diamond queen prematurely. Blocking the suit destroys the threat — keep Q83 in dummy as a connected menace.

hands.title: 226. Strip squeeze basics
hands.notes: Combine stripping with squeeze pressure. After the squeeze forces a discard, the defender is also endplayed.

hands.title: 227. Squeeze to produce an endplay
hands.notes: Run winners to squeeze West, then use the forced discard to create a throw-in position. Squeeze followed by endplay.

hands.title: 228. Double squeeze introduction
hands.notes: West guards hearts, East guards diamonds, both guard clubs. Running spades pressures both defenders in sequence — a double squeeze.

hands.title: 229. Threat cards and menace basics
hands.notes: Identify threat cards: the heart 6 threatens West's guard, the diamond Q threatens West's stopper. Menaces must be accompanied by entries.

hands.title: 230. Recognizing when a squeeze is impossible
hands.notes: No squeeze exists — threats lack entries, or both suits are guarded by different defenders with no common pressure point. Seek alternative play.
